title: JV-13 - Config default flip gated by benchmark and local-mt feature availability

labels.name: priority:P0
labels.description: Must-have for the first usable product increment

labels.name: level: atomic
labels.description: Small independent execution slice that can be assigned directly

body:
## Summary

Flip mt_provider default to local only after benchmark and runtime gates pass, preserving existing user configs.

## Why is this needed?

The product wants local MT as default, but premature default flip can break users or lower quality.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- Decision ADR from JV-08.
- Runtime/provider readiness from JV-11/JV-12.
- src/config/mod.rs default_mt_provider and config migration behavior.
- config.example.json and onboarding wizard.

## Outputs

- Gated default flip implementation.
- Migration tests preserving explicit google configs.
- Updated config example and onboarding choice semantics.

## Implementation approach

- Use cfg(feature='local-mt') or release build profile to avoid defaulting to local in binaries that cannot run it.
- Do not change existing config files that explicitly set google.
- Default mt_cloud_fallback remains None unless user opts in.
- Expose clear settings/onboarding affordance for Google fallback consent.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Fresh config in local-mt release defaults mt_provider=local.
- [ ] Fresh config in non-local-mt build does not default to unusable local provider.
- [ ] Existing mt_provider=google remains google after load/save.
- [ ] No cloud fallback is enabled by default.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] Config default tests for feature/no-feature paths.
- [ ] Load/save migration tests.
- [ ] config.example JSON validation.
- [ ] Onboarding/settings persistence tests.

## Dependencies

- Depends on JV-08, JV-11, JV-12, JV-15, JV-16.
- Should land after benchmark evidence is committed.

## Agent routing

Build: config specialist. Review: tui-rust-code-reviewer and nfr-verification-gate.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-13
- Wave: C1 config
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: feature, area: config, area: providers, provider: local, priority:P0, level: atomic

## Duplicate / extension note

Completes the conditional default flip that #372 intentionally left blocked.
